deck-repair.review_excerpt: Bill explained what could actually be repaired, what needed replacement, and what could wait. That honesty mattered.
proof_points: Real deck repair pages now map to one city and one service instead of generic catch-all copy.
The estimate path goes straight to first-party capture instead of sending people into someone else's marketplace.
Repair pages support the 'small projects welcome' positioning inside TonyOS.

fence-contractor.review_excerpt: We needed a practical fence fix, not a giant sales pitch. The estimate was clear and the job scope made sense.
proof_points: Fence pages focus on the jobs homeowners actually search for: repair, gates, storm damage, and replacement planning.
The local service page feeds the same quote system as the rest of the site, which keeps routing clean.
This lane supports fast-close exterior jobs, not just long-cycle remodel leads.

landscaping.review_excerpt: The cleanup and curb-appeal work made the whole place look better without turning it into a complicated project.
proof_points: Landscaping pages now support one-time cleanup, mulch, rock, trimming, and curb-appeal intent instead of vague lawn copy.
The page structure is useful for Google and for homeowners comparing cleanup versus larger upgrade work.
This lane gives All-Pro a practical path into seasonal local demand without relying on directory ads.

pressure-washing.review_excerpt: The concrete and outdoor surfaces looked better fast, and the whole job felt simple from estimate to cleanup.
proof_points: Pressure washing pages target fast, high-intent cleanup searches that can turn into easy-close homeowner leads.
Each page stores source, page, city, and campaign fields so the close rate can be measured by market.
This service lane also creates natural internal links into landscaping, deck repair, and yard cleanup.

yard-cleanup.review_excerpt: We had brush, debris, and cleanup work that kept getting pushed off. The estimate was straightforward and the property finally looked under control again.
proof_points: Yard cleanup pages support seasonal demand, storm cleanup, and neglected-property searches without pretending to be a giant landscaping firm.
This page cluster gives the site a cleaner entry point for homeowners who need smaller practical work done now.
Cleanup pages can be routed directly or bundled into larger exterior jobs later.

handyman.review_excerpt: We had a list of small repairs and no one wanted the job. All-Pro treated it like real work and got us moving again.
proof_points: Handyman pages reinforce the 'small projects welcome' message from TonyOS instead of burying those jobs.
This cluster captures punch-list demand that often gets lost when sites only push big remodel offers.
The form fields make it easier to tell small-repair leads apart from larger remodel traffic.

belleville.extra_note: Belleville pages are the clearest current revenue cluster and should keep getting the strongest project proof and photo depth.
belleville.intro_hook: This page is built for Belleville homeowners who want a real next step, not a city-name swap pasted onto generic contractor copy.

ofallon.extra_note: O'Fallon homeowners usually care about timeline, cleanliness, and getting fast outside work handled without drama.
ofallon.intro_hook: This page is built for O'Fallon homeowners who want outside work handled cleanly, quoted clearly, and scheduled without runaround.

edwardsville.extra_note: Edwardsville pages should lean into communication, curb appeal, and cleaner-finish positioning.
edwardsville.intro_hook: This page is built for Edwardsville homeowners who want a cleaner-finish contractor page with a direct, local estimate path.

collinsville.extra_note: Collinsville pages support practical repair and cleanup demand that often closes faster than long-cycle remodel work.
collinsville.intro_hook: This page is built for Collinsville homeowners who need practical outside work fixed before it spreads into a bigger mess.

swansea.extra_note: Swansea creates a useful hyperlocal lane for smaller jobs and fast-close homeowner cleanup demand.
swansea.intro_hook: This page is built for Swansea homeowners who want a smaller local job taken seriously instead of being ignored because it is not a giant remodel.

pressure-washing::belleville.extra_note: Belleville pressure washing queries are one of the easiest ways to turn cleanup intent into broader outdoor project discovery.
